name: Ja'far ibn Ali
title: Brother's Sacrifice
shortDescription: The 19-year-old youth and another brave son of Umm al-Banin (SA) who entered the battlefield after his brothers and created an Alawi epic.
fullStory:
Ja'far ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib was the youngest son of Lady Umm al-Banin (SA) and Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). Amir al-Mu'minin named him in memory of his martyred brother 'Ja'far al-Tayyar'. He was only 19 years old on the day of Ashura, but a spirit as grand as a mountain and Haidari bravery surged within him.

When Hazrat Abbas (AS) called upon his brothers to go to the battlefield and defend Imam Hussain (AS), Ja'far, with a heart full of faith and without a speck of fear, prepared for battle. After the martyrdom of his brothers Abdullah and Uthman, with the permission of his older brother Abbas (AS) and tearful eyes from the loss of his brothers, he entered the battlefield.

Ja'far began reciting war poetry in the field, saying with a resounding voice: 'I am Ja'far, the possessor of great honors; the son of Ali, the conqueror of Khaybar...'. Despite his young age, he wielded his sword with such power that it reminded the Kufan army of his father Ali's (AS) bravery in the Battle of Siffin.

After a fierce battle and killing a number of enemies, Hani ibn Thubayt al-Hadrami (who was also the killer of his brother Abdullah) cowardly attacked him from behind and martyred this young Alawi youth with a sword strike.

The martyrdom of Ja'far added another golden page to the honors of Umm al-Banin's (SA) family. This devoted mother, by offering her four valiant sons in one day, eternalized her name in history as the unparalleled symbol of loyalty and devotion to Wilayah.
quoteArabic: إِنّي أَنَا جَعْفَرٌ ذُو المَعالي، نَجْلُ عَلِيٍّ الخَيِّرِ المِفْضالِ
quote: I am Ja'far, the possessor of great honors; the son of Ali, the righteous and generous man.
